event: history of redness, pain, and swelling
associated with fever, chills, and vomiting
treated with intravenous vancomycin for cellulitis
left inner thigh and groin induration
morbidly obese with a BMI>45 kg/m2
LRINEC score of 6, C-reactive protein (CRP) excluded
positive subcutaneous thickening, air, and fascial fluid (STAFF)
started on intravenous vancomycin and piperacillin/tazobactam
operative debridement of the left groin and perineum
excision of 15 cm × 23 cm of tissue
admitted to the surgical intensive care unit post-operatively
septic shock requiring vasopressors and ventilator dependence
repeat washouts with minor debridements daily for 3 days
extubated and transferred to a step-down unit
plastic surgery was consulted to evaluate for possible skin graft
transferred to the plastic surgery service
forgo skin graft during her immediate hospital stay
fully ambulatory and discharged home with a wound V.A.C.
